summary: In this episode, Bhartendra sits down with Amit Bivalkar, Founder of Sapient Finserv (now in partnership with Equirus), for one of the most candid conversations we've had on this show. They unpack what's actually driving the FII-retail tug-of-war, the exact 60-15-15-10 asset allocation Amit recommends for a 35-year-old today, why gold is insurance and not a return engine, the active vs passive debate in the Indian context, and the one number Indian inve...

seo.title: Your SIP Isn’t the Problem, You Are
seo.description: In this episode, Bhartendra sits down with Amit Bivalkar, Founder of Sapient Finserv (now in partnership with Equirus), for one of the most candid

takeaways.text: The biggest threat to your SIP is your own behaviour, not the market, which is exactly why an automated SIP removes the most dangerous variable: you.
FIIs behave like tourists who come and go, so their selling is not a reason to abandon a plan built for your own life.
A 10 to 12% long-term return is something to be genuinely happy about, not disappointed by.
Amit's 60-15-15-10 framework treats gold as insurance rather than a return engine, and is built for a 15-year horizon.
Your savings rate is the one number most Indian investors refuse to track, and 30% is meant to be the floor, not the goal.
